title: How Do I Know If a Seller Is Genuine?
metaDescription: Wondering how do I know if a seller is genuine when buying a car? Our guide helps you spot red flags and buy with confidence from genuine sellers at BuyAndScrap.com.
targetKeyword: how do i know if a seller is genuine
content:
Buying a used car can be exciting, but concerns about seller authenticity are common. "How do I know if a seller is genuine?" is a frequent question. At BuyAndScrap.com, we help connect genuine buyers and sellers, but knowing what to look out for yourself is always smart.

## The Importance of Due Diligence

Taking time and doing homework before parting with cash is crucial. It protects your investment and ensures you get what you pay for. A genuine seller is usually transparent and happy to answer questions.

## Key Checks Before You Buy

### Check the Advert Carefully
Start with the listing. Does the description match photos? Are there plenty of pictures? Be wary of limited information, blurry photos, or prices that seem too good to be true – they often are. Inconsistencies or typos can be subtle red flags.

### Communicate Directly with the Seller
Don't be shy about asking questions: car history, reasons for selling, known issues. Pay attention to how the seller responds. Are they open, or evasive? Observing their responses helps you gauge how do I know if a seller is genuine. Clear communication is a good sign.

### View the Car in Person
Always arrange to see the car in person, ideally during daylight and at the seller's home address (matching the V5C). Avoid secluded car parks. Check the car's condition against the advert and ask the seller to show you their ID and the V5C (logbook) to confirm they are the registered keeper.

### Documentation is King
Insist on seeing all relevant paperwork: V5C logbook, service history, MOT certificates, repair invoices. Cross-reference V5C details with the car (VIN, registration plate). A complete and consistent history strongly indicates a genuine sale.

### The Test Drive
Never skip a test drive. Ensure the seller is insured for you to drive, or have temporary cover. Listen for unusual noises, check brakes, steering, and all electrics. This is your chance to assess the car’s condition beyond its looks.

## What If Something Feels Off?

Trust your gut. If a seller pressures you, refuses to show documents, insists on meeting in an unusual location, or has a convoluted story, walk away. Ultimately, when asking yourself how do I know if a seller is genuine, look for transparency, clear communication, and a willingness to provide all necessary information. If these elements are missing, it’s best to err on the side of caution.

Buying a car should be a smooth experience. By following these simple tips, you'll be well-equipped to spot genuine sellers and drive away happy.
